stories.title: Antonelli Wins Belgian Grand Prix at Spa

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
ANTONELLI WINS THE BELGIAN GRAND PRIX. 🏆

Pole to victory at Spa.

P2 Leclerc. P3 Verstappen. P4 Hamilton (5-sec penalty).
Russell: retired LAP 1.

Kimi Antonelli is 45 points clear in the title race.

#F1 #BelgianGP

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Kimi Antonelli in 2026:

✅ Wins: Belgian GP (today), + more
✅ Championship lead: 45 points
✅ Age: 19

The youngest F1 title favorite in a generation is running away with this.

#F1 #Antonelli #BelgianGP

article.headline: Antonelli Wins Belgian Grand Prix, Extends Championship Lead to 45 Points

stories.title: Russell Retires Lap 1 — The Collision That Could Define the Title Race

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
George Russell's Belgian GP ended on LAP 1.

Contact with Lewis Hamilton at Les Combes braking zone. Hamilton got a 5-second penalty.

Russell cited a power deployment issue that pushed him wide.

The title race just got a LOT clearer.

#F1 #BelgianGP #GeorgeRussell

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Hamilton called the Spa incident "a racing incident."

The stewards called it a penalty.

Russell is now 50 POINTS behind Antonelli in the championship.

Belgium may be the race that decided 2026.

#F1 #BelgianGP #Hamilton

article.headline: Russell Out on Lap 1: The Teammate Collision That Could Decide the 2026 F1 Title

stories.title: Verstappen Exit Clause — Now Officially Eligible

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Max Verstappen is P7 in the F1 championship.

His Red Bull exit clause can trigger in October if he's outside the top 2 at the summer break.

He's 7th. The clause is eligible.

When asked for an update at Spa: "No."

#F1 #Verstappen #RedBull

article.headline: Verstappen's Red Bull Exit Clause Is Now Eligible. Will He Use It?

research.facts:
Verstappen Exit Clause
**Source 1:** RacingNews365 — exit clause can be triggered in October if Verstappen is outside top 2 at summer break; he's now 7th
**Source 2:** GPFans — at Belgian GP press conference, Verstappen gave flat "No" when asked for update
**Source 3:** Manager Raymond Vermeulen: said Verstappen "wants to finish with Red Bull"; clause existing "doesn't mean they'll invoke it"
**Source 4:** PlanetF1, SportBible — McLaren talks "in closing stages"
**Source 5:** ESPN — pushed back on McLaren rumours, called them "wide of the mark"
**Confidence:** HIGH on clause eligibility (standings confirmed); MEDIUM on McLaren deal being close (conflicting sources)

stories.title: Title Fight Analysis — Antonelli's Path to the Championship

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
F1 2026 title standings after Belgium:

1. Antonelli (Mercedes)
2. Hamilton — 45 pts back
3. Russell — 50 pts back
4. Leclerc — 78 pts back

Three races. Three McLaren rivals chasing. One teenage champion pulling away.

#F1 #Formula1

article.headline: Forty-Five Points Clear: How Antonelli Turned a Three-Way Fight Into a Title March

stories.title: Spa by the Numbers — Race Strategy and Key Moments

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Things that shaped the Belgian GP result:

→ Antonelli: pole, perfect race execution
→ Leclerc: lost the lead in a VSC window
→ Norris: started P13 (grid penalty)
→ Russell: out lap 1
→ Hamilton: 5-sec penalty, dropped to P4

Spa decides everything.

#F1 #BelgianGP

article.headline: How the 2026 Belgian GP Was Won: Antonelli's Dominant Spa and the Moments That Defined It

research.facts:
Race Strategy/Spa Context
**Source:** Various race reports (formula1.com, Sky Sports, GPFans)
- Norris started P13 (10-place grid penalty — 4th electronics unit)
- Stroll P20, Hadjar P21, Alonso P22 (also penalized)
- Antonelli's pole: 1:44.361 (largest margin of 2026 season)
- Leclerc lost lead during VSC window — strategy call that didn't pay off
- Hamilton called incident with Russell "a racing incident"
**Confidence:** HIGH on all Spa strategy facts
